results.message: I could not apply the service_recovery discount to basket_025. The basket subtotal is 13600 cents, so the largest policy-allowed service_recovery discount would be 5%, but I cannot verify that the basket is checkoutable because the workspace does not expose the required inventory/store data for store_bratislava_stare_mesto. Without that checkoutability check, /docs/discounts.md forbids running /bin/discount.

I could not apply the service_recovery discount. I resolved the last basket from your store as /proc/baskets/basket_025.json for customer /proc/customers/cust_037.json, and its single line item totals 13,600 cents (2 × 6,800), which would cap the discount at 5% if eligible. But /docs/discounts.md requires every basket line to be checkoutable under /docs/checkout.md, and that requires store inventory verification. In this workspace, /bin/sql reports that no SQLite catalogue is available, and /AGENTS.MD states inventory exists only in SQL projections, so I cannot verify checkoutability and therefore cannot safely run /bin/discount.

I couldn’t apply the discount. I verified the last in-scope basket is /proc/baskets/basket_025.json for this customer, and its subtotal is 13,600 cents, so the largest allowed `service_recovery` discount would be 5% if the basket were checkoutable. But the discount policy requires every basket line to be checkoutable, and the checkout policy requires inventory evidence for the basket’s store. In this workspace, `/bin/sql` has no catalogue DB available and no store/inventory records are exposed under `/proc`, so I can’t verify checkoutability and must not run `/bin/discount`.
